summary: Tested all 6 iter91 fixes: email tenant_id fix, product visibility (blacklist/whitelist), pricing on store cards, signup redesign, and ProductForm 3-mode visibility UI. Found and fixed 1 critical bug: AdminProductUpdate model was missing 'restricted_to' field causing 500 on PUT /api/admin/products/{id}. All 12 backend tests pass. All frontend tests pass.

critical.issue: AdminProductUpdate model was missing 'restricted_to: Optional[List[str]] = None' field, causing AttributeError 500. FIXED by adding the field to AdminProductUpdate in models.py

action_items: FIXED (in this iteration): Add 'restricted_to: Optional[List[str]] = None' to AdminProductUpdate model in models.py - was causing 500 on PUT /api/admin/products/{id}
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: AdminProductUpdate model was missing restricted_to field while AdminProductCreate had it - this caused PUT endpoint to 500 with AttributeError. Fix applied in /app/backend/models.py
Email fix verified: EmailService.send() now correctly gets from_email from resend_settings (not credentials) at line 379
Auth fix verified: POST /api/auth/register passes tenant_id=tenant_id to EmailService.send() at line 902
Store visibility logic verified: restricted_to works as blacklist, visible_to_customers as whitelist, admins bypass both checks
seed_data_creation: Created test customer + two products (restricted and whitelist) via fixtures; cleaned up by deactivating products after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 iter91 fixes verified working. Key fix applied: AdminProductUpdate model in models.py now has restricted_to field. Backend: visibility filtering (blacklist/whitelist + admin bypass) fully tested. Frontend: signup split-panel layout works, store cards show pricing (from $X format), ProductForm shows 3 radio visibility modes correctly (All customers / Restrict from specific / Show only to specific). Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter91_visibility_email_fixes.py

rca_of_issue.bug: PUT /api/admin/products/{id} returned 500 when updating a product with restricted_to field
rca_of_issue.root_cause: AdminProductUpdate Pydantic model in /app/backend/models.py did not include the 'restricted_to' field, while AdminProductCreate did have it. The catalog.py update handler (line 257) tried to access payload.restricted_to which caused AttributeError since the field didn't exist on the model.
rca_of_issue.fix: Added 'restricted_to: Optional[List[str]] = None' to AdminProductUpdate class in /app/backend/models.py
rca_of_issue.reproduction: POST /api/admin/products to create product, then PUT /api/admin/products/{id} with restricted_to in body => was giving 500 AttributeError
